name: ◁ Turkish Cheese BörekPHYLLO CHEESE PIE
servings: prep time: 20 minutes, plus 2 hours thawing

ingredients.item:
PHYLLO PASTRY
1 package (1 pound, or 454 g) frozen phyllo dough (16 to 20 sheets)
¼ teaspoon nigella seeds
ingredients.original:
PHYLLO PASTRY
1 package (1 pound, or 454 g) frozen phyllo dough (16 to 20 sheets)
¼ teaspoon nigella seeds

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon white sesame seeds
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon white sesame seeds

ingredients.item:
CHEESE FILLING
4½ ounces (130 g) crumbled feta cheese
12/3 cups (185 g) shredded mozzarella cheese
ingredients.original:
CHEESE FILLING
4½ ounces (130 g) crumbled feta cheese
12/3 cups (185 g) shredded mozzarella cheese

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (25 g) grated Parmesan cheese (or use more feta)
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (25 g) grated Parmesan cheese (or use more feta)

ingredients.item: ½ cup (25 g) finely chopped fresh parsley
ingredients.original: ½ cup (25 g) finely chopped fresh parsley

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (40 g) finely chopped white onion (or substitute green onions)
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (40 g) finely chopped white onion (or substitute green onions)

ingredients.item: 1 teaspoon nigella seeds
ingredients.original: 1 teaspoon nigella seeds

ingredients.item:
EGG WASH
3 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted
½ cup (120 ml) olive oil
ingredients.original:
EGG WASH
3 tablespoons unsalted butter, melted
½ cup (120 ml) olive oil

ingredients.item: 1 large egg
ingredients.original: 1 large egg

ingredients.item: ¼ cup (60 ml) whole milk
ingredients.original: ¼ cup (60 ml) whole milk

ingredients.item: 1 TO PREPARE THE PHYLLO PASTRY: Bring the phyllo dough to room temperature by thawing on the counter for 2 hours or in the refrigerator overnight.
ingredients.original: 1 TO PREPARE THE PHYLLO PASTRY: Bring the phyllo dough to room temperature by thawing on the counter for 2 hours or in the refrigerator overnight.

ingredients.item: 2 Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) on the convection setting (if you do not have this setting, use the bake setting) and place an oven rack in the middle.
ingredients.original: 2 Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C) on the convection setting (if you do not have this setting, use the bake setting) and place an oven rack in the middle.

ingredients.item: 3 TO MAKE THE CHEESE FILLING: In a medium bowl, mix together the feta, mozzarella, and Parmesan cheeses. Add the parsley, onion, and 1 teaspoon nigella seeds and mix well to combine.
ingredients.original: 3 TO MAKE THE CHEESE FILLING: In a medium bowl, mix together the feta, mozzarella, and Parmesan cheeses. Add the parsley, onion, and 1 teaspoon nigella seeds and mix well to combine.

ingredients.item: 4 TO MAKE THE EGG WASH: In a small bowl, whisk together the melted butter, oil, egg, and milk until combined.
ingredients.original: 4 TO MAKE THE EGG WASH: In a small bowl, whisk together the melted butter, oil, egg, and milk until combined.

ingredients.item: 5 Open the phyllo dough package and lay the pastry sheets on a flat surface, loosely covered with a kitchen towel.
ingredients.original: 5 Open the phyllo dough package and lay the pastry sheets on a flat surface, loosely covered with a kitchen towel.

ingredients.item: 6 Cut a piece of parchment paper that fits the dimensions of a 10 x 12-inch (25 x 30 cm) baking dish and place it inside the dish. Use a pastry brush to brush the bottom and sides of the dish with egg wash, right over the parchment paper. Place 2 to 4 phyllo sheets on the base of the dish, allowing the pastry to overhang all sides, so there are at least 2 inches (5 cm) of excess dough. Place the sheets slightly offset from each other so that there is enough dough overhanging; this excess overhang will be folded over the topmost layer at the end to ensure the filling stays securely inside and does not ooze out. Brush these sheets with egg wash until well saturated.
ingredients.original: 6 Cut a piece of parchment paper that fits the dimensions of a 10 x 12-inch (25 x 30 cm) baking dish and place it inside the dish. Use a pastry brush to brush the bottom and sides of the dish with egg wash, right over the parchment paper. Place 2 to 4 phyllo sheets on the base of the dish, allowing the pastry to overhang all sides, so there are at least 2 inches (5 cm) of excess dough. Place the sheets slightly offset from each other so that there is enough dough overhanging; this excess overhang will be folded over the topmost layer at the end to ensure the filling stays securely inside and does not ooze out. Brush these sheets with egg wash until well saturated.

ingredients.item: 7 Set aside 2 phyllo sheets to use for the top of the börek. Divide the remaining sheets in half. Use half of the sheets for the next few steps to layer the börek. Pick up one sheet at a time, tear it into 2 or 3 pieces, and scrunch up the pieces with your hands to create crumpled-up dough (like you would crumple up a piece of paper). Place the crumpled-up dough pieces loosely in the dish, spreading them out evenly across the surface area; crumpling the pastry will create air pockets for a softer, lighter börek.
ingredients.original: 7 Set aside 2 phyllo sheets to use for the top of the börek. Divide the remaining sheets in half. Use half of the sheets for the next few steps to layer the börek. Pick up one sheet at a time, tear it into 2 or 3 pieces, and scrunch up the pieces with your hands to create crumpled-up dough (like you would crumple up a piece of paper). Place the crumpled-up dough pieces loosely in the dish, spreading them out evenly across the surface area; crumpling the pastry will create air pockets for a softer, lighter börek.

ingredients.item: 8 Use the pastry brush to gently brush egg wash all over the crumpled phyllo sheets, then spoon all the cheese mixture into the baking dish, on top of the crumpled-up dough, creating an even layer.
ingredients.original: 8 Use the pastry brush to gently brush egg wash all over the crumpled phyllo sheets, then spoon all the cheese mixture into the baking dish, on top of the crumpled-up dough, creating an even layer.

ingredients.item: 9 Repeat the same tearing, scrunching, and crumpling of the dough with the remaining half of the phyllo sheets, then place them over the cheese mixture in an even layer and brush them with egg wash.
ingredients.original: 9 Repeat the same tearing, scrunching, and crumpling of the dough with the remaining half of the phyllo sheets, then place them over the cheese mixture in an even layer and brush them with egg wash.

ingredients.item: 10 Fold the overhanging phyllo dough over the topmost layer and pat down gently to flatten. Place the reserved 2 sheets on top in a smooth, flat layer; this layer should cover the full surface of the börek. If you must trim some excess dough to create an even layer, do so. Brush the top with the remaining egg wash.
ingredients.original: 10 Fold the overhanging phyllo dough over the topmost layer and pat down gently to flatten. Place the reserved 2 sheets on top in a smooth, flat layer; this layer should cover the full surface of the börek. If you must trim some excess dough to create an even layer, do so. Brush the top with the remaining egg wash.

ingredients.item: 11 Using a sharp knife, gently cut the börek into 12 equal-size squares, using a sawing motion to avoid disturbing the phyllo dough too much and to keep the pieces intact. Sprinkle the surface with the ¼ teaspoon nigella seeds and the sesame seeds.
ingredients.original: 11 Using a sharp knife, gently cut the börek into 12 equal-size squares, using a sawing motion to avoid disturbing the phyllo dough too much and to keep the pieces intact. Sprinkle the surface with the ¼ teaspoon nigella seeds and the sesame seeds.

ingredients.item: 12 Place in the oven on the middle rack and bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the filling is heated through and melted. You may broil the top for a few minutes, if required, to achieve a golden color. Let cool for 10 minutes. Use a sharp knife to gently slice into the precut squares and serve.
ingredients.original: 12 Place in the oven on the middle rack and bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until the top is golden brown and the filling is heated through and melted. You may broil the top for a few minutes, if required, to achieve a golden color. Let cool for 10 minutes. Use a sharp knife to gently slice into the precut squares and serve.
